Barak Ohana

About Me

I am a PhD student at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem supervised by Prof. Zlil Sela and Prof. Chloé Perin.

Before that, I've completed my master's degree at the Hebrew University under the supervision of Prof. Chloé Perin.

My mathematical fields of interest include geometric group theory; more specifically, I am interested in hierarchically hyperbolic groups (and especially mapping class groups).

Contact: barak.ohana (at) mail.huji.ac.il

Preprint & Projects

  • On Equational Noetherianity of Colorable Hierarchically Hyperbolic Groups arXiv:2410.00977
  • In work: Algebraic Closure in Free Groups with Imaginaries (revision needed, master thesis) draft

Research Talks

  • Equations over Hierarchically Hyperbolic Groups, Groups and Dynamics Seminar, The Hebrew University, December 2024.
  • JSJ Theory and its usage in Model Theory, Séminaire de théorie ergodique, Institut de recherche mathématique de Rennes, May 2022.

Prizes and Honors

  • Recipient of the Dr. Morris Tenenbaum Award, 2021
